title: Model card and human oversight deterministic AI draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, evidence-boundary fill-gap for Model card and human oversight; all items remain blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Model card and human oversight: reject the overclaim
items.prompt: Which statement is source-bounded and corrects this AI misconception? Publishing a model card makes a model transparent, fair, and safe even when evidence is incomplete or deployment changes.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Choose the statement that names mechanism, evidence conditions, and the boundary of the claim rather than a fluent or benchmark-based overgeneralization.

feedback.correct: Correct. Keep model-card claims traceable to versioned evidence, name exclusions and uncertainty, and connect oversight, monitoring, escalation, and update ownership.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the overclaim. Keep model-card claims traceable to versioned evidence, name exclusions and uncertainty, and connect oversight, monitoring, escalation, and update ownership.

criteria.description: Selects the source-bounded correction and rejects the documented AI overclaim.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against cited sources and ensure the distractor does not normalize unsafe or overstated claims.

options.text: Keep model-card claims traceable to versioned evidence, name exclusions and uncertainty, and connect oversight, monitoring, escalation, and update ownership.
Publishing a model card makes a model transparent, fair, and safe even when evidence is incomplete or deployment changes.

answer.explanation: Keep model-card claims traceable to versioned evidence, name exclusions and uncertainty, and connect oversight, monitoring, escalation, and update ownership.

items.title: Model card and human oversight: canonical evidence boundary
items.prompt: Complete the exact source-bounded correction phrase; the complete sentence must remain an honest beginner-to-intermediate claim.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint from the correction and ensure it narrows rather than expands the evidence claim.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'traceable to versioned evidence'. Keep model-card claims traceable to versioned evidence, name exclusions and uncertainty, and connect oversight, monitoring, escalation, and update ownership.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the technical and responsible-AI boundary. Keep model-card claims traceable to versioned evidence, name exclusions and uncertainty, and connect oversight, monitoring, escalation, and update ownership.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical phrase that completes the bounded source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and technically equivalent variants before learner use.
items.template: Keep model-card claims [[checkpoint]], name exclusions and uncertainty, and connect oversight, monitoring, escalation, and update ownership.

answer.explanation: Keep model-card claims traceable to versioned evidence, name exclusions and uncertainty, and connect oversight, monitoring, escalation, and update ownership.
